remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Enterprise AI transformation: how leading organizations are redesigning operations around AI
remarks.content: Most enterprises have run AI pilots. But few have transformed their operations with AI at scale. What separates them is organizational design, not technology: disconnected people, disconnected systems, disconnected governance. According to "Global AI confessions report: data lead
remarks.contentTranslation: 大多数企业都运行AI试点。但很少有人用AI大规模地改变了他们的运营。将它们区分开来的是组织设计，而不是技术：断开的人员、断开的系统、断开的治理。根据《全球AI认罪报告：数据线索
remarks.translation: 企业AI转型：领先组织如何围绕AI重新设计运营
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text: From partnership to pipeline: how Snowflake and Dataiku drove 200%+ YoY sourced opportunity growth
remarks.content: Partnerships are often judged too early, or on the wrong things. Visibility. Executive alignment. Event presence. A well-worded press release. None of that is the real test.
remarks.contentTranslation: 人们往往过早地判断伙伴关系，或者判断错误。可见性。高管一致性。事件存在。措辞良好的新闻稿。这些都不是真正的考验。
remarks.translation: 从合作伙伴关系到渠道： Snowflake和Dataiku如何推动200% +同比机会增长

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: How enterprise analytics platforms are evolving: from dashboards to AI-driven decisions
remarks.content: The enterprise analytics platforms of 2023 were dashboard factories. The ones built for 2026 are decision engines where AI agents, machine learning (ML) models, and business rules operate alongside traditional reporting. But many enterprises still cannot route model outputs into decision
remarks.contentTranslation: 2023年的企业分析平台是仪表板工厂。为2026年构建的决策引擎是AIAgent、机器学习(ML)模型和业务规则与传统报告一起运行的决策引擎。但许多企业仍然无法将模型输出转化为决策
remarks.translation: 企业分析平台如何发展：从仪表板到AI驱动的决策

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
There are SO many people, friends, acquaintances, followers, startups, asking me to try their new AI products every day

Like I get at least 3 such requests per day

If I tried all of these products I wouldn’t have time to do anything else 

There are too many builders and the competition for attention is insane
remarks.translation: 每天都有很多人、朋友、熟人、追随者、初创公司要求我尝试他们的新AI产品，就像我每天至少收到3个这样的请求一样如果我尝试了所有这些产品，我就没有时间做任何其他事情了有太多的开发者，争夺注意力的竞争是疯狂的
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Peter Steinberger
remarks.role: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world.
remarks.text:
I can barely keep up with implementing/testing/landing all the Issues/PRs folks submit to https://t.co/qUxn0LF7Ct

Codex runs INSIDE crabbox while it is building crabbox.
This is becoming essential infra for my work.

Codex been looping nonstop for the last 4 days in multiple trees. Since all of it is e2e verifiable it basically builds itself. Codex even signs up for the services automatically via browser/computer use. My main job is adding credit card details and closing things that I don't see as a fit.
remarks.translation:
我几乎无法跟上实施/测试/登陆所有的问题/公关人员提交给Crabbox内部的Codex运行，而它正在构建Crabbox。这对我的工作至关重要。在过去的4天里，Codex在多棵树上不间断地循环。由于所有这些都是e2e可验证的，因此它基本上是自己构建的。Codex甚至通过浏览器/计算机使用自动注册服务。
我的主要工作是添加信用卡详细信息，并关闭我认为不合适的东西。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Amjad Masad
remarks.role: ceo replit. civilizationist
remarks.text:
When the whole Tokenmaxxing craze started some our enterprise customers asked us for a leaderboard.

We said no.

Would’ve been “great” for business but we’re not in the business of selling tokens for the sake of tokens.

We sell outcomes.

And we knew it wouldn’t last: https://t.co/LKwOkfZQm0
remarks.translation: 当整个Tokenmaxxing热潮开始时，我们的一些企业客户要求我们获得排行榜。我们说不。这对企业来说是“伟大的” ，但我们不是为了代币而出售代币。我们销售成果。我们知道它不会持久：
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
## The Future Codebase

After the PR dies, after the Code Review dies,
i am seriously wondering if Git needs to die next.

roughly 20-40% of code spend is just managing and updating merge conflicts. necessary evil? or legacy "horseless carriage"? cargo culting the past?

we don't do line by line merge conflicts when we collaborate with human colleagues - instead we chat, suggest edits, do side comments, and an owner ships it. 

btw we also don't do CI/CD even collaborating on documents with serious legal/financial implications.

maybe the future codebase looks more like a Notion or Linear database than .git objects. It will be less efficient, but more scalable. exactly the Salty Lesson.
remarks.translation:
# #未来的代码库公关结束后，代码审查结束后，我真的很想知道Git接下来是否需要死亡。大约20-40%的代码花费只是管理和更新合并冲突。必要的邪恶？或遗留的“无马车” ？过去的货物？当我们与人类同事合作时，我们不会逐行合并冲突-相反，我们会聊天，建议编辑，做附带评论，所有者会发送它。
顺便说一句，我们甚至不对具有严重法律/财务影响的文档进行CI/CD协作。也许未来的代码库看起来更像是一个概念或线性数据库，而不是.git对象。它将效率较低，但可扩展性更强。正是Salty Lesson。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
Just wrote out a whole doc with my bare hands - manually, with a keyboard. 

No dictation, no AI. 

Because I like to live dangerously.
remarks.translation: 我赤手空拳写了一整篇文档--用手写，用键盘。没有听写，没有AI。因为我喜欢危险的生活。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
We just shipped 𝙷𝚊𝚛𝚗𝚎𝚜𝚜𝙰𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝, a unified abstraction to orchestrate and integrate any agent’s “brain” into your app.

@aisdk now frees you from both model and agent lock-in. (And it doesn’t just get you portability, it’s also delightful to use ofc!) https://t.co/CTbttpPKp8
remarks.translation: 我们刚刚推出了一个统一的抽象，用于编排任何Agent的“大脑”并将其集成到您的应用程序中。@ aisdk现在将您从模型和Agent锁定中解放出来。（它不仅让您轻松携带，而且使用ofc也很愉快！ ）
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
neat thing about developer exception engineering is:

the happy paths are all happy in their own way.

the unhappy paths are ~universally the same.

https://t.co/Wnbtc9eAzk
remarks.translation: 开发人员异常工程的整洁之处在于：快乐的路径都以自己的方式快乐。不快乐的路径~普遍相同。

remarks.text:
Fable feels superhuman at working over long agentic conversations, sometimes to the point where I can't keep up with what it's telling me 😅

This prompt snippet has been the best fix I've found for getting it to write clearly and drop any jargon: https://t.co/9mS57mR7jq

remarks.text: In awe of SpaceX and its story - past, present and the future. You can think about it in 10+ different ways and continue re-blowing your mind in circles. Huge congrats to the team! 🚀

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
Anyone else have two voices?

I often have two voices that come out both in my writing and how I speak. 

One is the frenetic, time is the enemy, direct, punchy gets to the point quickly and then the second is more calm, measured, structured inquisitive, trying to peel the layers. 

They are distinctive enough that people think it’s two different people (especially in writing). 

They are usually separate but I feel I often oscillate between the two in the same conversation.
remarks.translation:
还有谁有两个声音？在我的写作和说话方式中，我经常有两个声音。一个是狂热的，时间是敌人，直接的，有冲击力的很快就到了重点，然后第二个是更平静，测量，结构化的好奇，试图剥离层。它们足够独特，以至于人们认为这是两个不同的人（尤其是在写作中）。
它们通常是分开的，但我觉得我经常在同一对话中在两者之间摇摆。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.translation: 难以置信。恭喜@ elonmusk和整个SpaceX团队在25年的热血、汗水和泪水中建立了一家定义世界的公司。能有这样的例子来推动未来，真是太棒了。其下游影响是巨大的。

remarks.name: Garry Tan
remarks.role: President & CEO ycombinator —Founder garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
remarks.text:
I just discovered forceBlockStreamingForReasoning = resolvedReasoningLevel === "on" for OpenClaw and frankly I love it

Seeing the reasoning traces of my claw with Claude Fable 5 is a mind-blowing experience. Seeing the tale of the tape, and being able to engage with ideas at a deeper level has always been my catnip.
remarks.translation: 我刚刚发现OpenClaw的forceBlockStreamingForReasoning = resolvedReasoningLevel = = = "on" ，坦率地说，我喜欢它看到我与Claude Fable 5的推理痕迹是一种令人兴奋的体验。看到录像带的故事，并能够在更深层次上与想法互动，一直是我的猫薄荷。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Garry Tan
remarks.role: President & CEO ycombinator —Founder garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
remarks.text:
Everyone thinks AI coding tools set founders free.

Watch what people actually build with them: rules, approvals, process, layers. The same cage, assembled faster.

The tool that can scaffold anything in an afternoon will scaffold your bureaucracy in an afternoon too.

Speed of construction is speed of calcification. Build the thing that lets you create new things: experiences that didn’t happen before.
remarks.translation: 每个人都认为AI编码工具可以让创始人获得自由。观察人们实际使用它们构建的内容：规则、审批、流程、层次。同样的笼子，组装得更快。可以在一个下午支撑任何东西的工具也会在一个下午支撑你的官僚机构。构造的速度就是钙化的速度。打造能让您创造新事物的体验：前所未有的体验。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。
